response.justification: The candidate is a strong and versatile web/full-stack engineer with nearly two decades of experience, but he does not meet the core requirements of this role. He lacks native-level Japanese proficiency (a hard requirement), shows no Linux kernel investigation or source-modification experience, and has no background in L2/L3 networking, switch products, or security technologies such as MACsec or 802.1X. His C experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments a decade ago, whereas the role demands strong current C proficiency for UNIX embedded development.
strengths: Extensive overall software development experience (15+ years) across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Systems-level performance work at Dunnhumby (low-latency services, async I/O, high-throughput infrastructure)
Some C/C++ experience at National Instruments and demonstrated Linux familiarity
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency, which is a stated hard requirement
No Linux kernel, embedded firmware, network switch, or security technology (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF) experience
C is not a current or primary skill; recent decade is dominated by TypeScript/Node.js web development

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or Linux kernel background
Strong general engineering experience and systems exposure
